description: Lock carbon-accounting software as a post-standards GHG inventory tooling layer, not a prerequisite for older GHG inventory or CCS technologies.

checks.reason: GHG Protocol anchors the modern corporate GHG accounting standard and related calculation-tool lineage around the 2001 Corporate Standard.
GHG inventory standards and reporting practice precede the software tools that implement them.
Inventory estimation and uncertainty handling still rely on statistical inference.
Environmental accounting context remains a foundation for GHG inventory practice.
Carbon accounting is reporting context for CCS, not a physical prerequisite for capture and storage systems.
CCS still depends on chemistry for gas separation and process control.
Geologic sequestration still depends on deep well and subsurface storage capabilities.
Direct air capture should inherit from physical CCS, not from carbon-accounting reporting software.
